channel.title: The Batch
channel.description: Weekly AI news for engineers, executives, and enthusiasts.

item.title: GPT-5.4 Makes A Splash, AI’s Growth on Mobile, Data Centers Go Off-Grid, Apple’s Diffusion Research
item.description: The Batch AI News and Insights: Should there be a Stack Overflow for AI coding agents to share their learnings with each other?

item.title: Attacks On Data Centers, Qwen3.5 In All Sizes, DeepSeek’s Huawei Play, Apple’s Multimodal Tokenizer
item.description: The Batch AI News and Insights: I’ve been hearing from people at all levels of seniority about a feeling of job insecurity.

item.title: Nvidia’s Open Salvo, OpenAI’s Amazon Deal, Grok Cuts Video Prices, Recursive Language Models
item.description: The Batch AI News and Insights: The anti-AI coalition continues to maneuver to find arguments to slow down AI progress.

item.title: Claude Code’s Source Leaks, OpenAI Exits Video Generation, Gemini Adds Music Generation, LLMs Learn at Inference
item.description: The Batch AI News and Insights: Voice-based AI that you can talk to is improving rapidly, yet most people still don’t appreciate how pervasive voice UIs (user interfaces) will become.

item.title: Anthropic’s Claude Mythos Problem, Dark DNA Unveiled, Pitfalls for Assistive Models, Simulating Fluid Dynamics
item.description: The Batch AI News and Insights: As AI agents accelerate coding, what is the future of software engineering?

item.title: Meta Pivots From Open Weights, Big Pharma Bets On AI, Regulatory Patchwork, Simulating Human Cohorts
item.description: The Batch AI News and Insights: AI-native software engineering teams operate very differently than traditional teams.

item.title: GLM 5.1 Thinks Strategically, Data-Center Revolt Intensifies, When Helpful LLMs Turn Unhelpful, Humanoid Robots Get to Work
item.description: The Batch AI News and Insights: Coding agents are accelerating different types of software work to different degrees.

item.title: GPT-5.5 Outperforms (and Hallucinates), Kimi K2.6 Leads Open LLMs, AI Strains Climate Pledges, Strategic Thinking in LLMs vs. Humans
item.description: The Batch AI News and Insights: The ways we prompt AI are very different in 2026 than 2022 when ChatGPT came out.

item.title: Seedance Makes A Splash, Nvidia's AI-Guided Chip Designs, Helping Robots Not Forget
item.description: The Batch AI News and Insights: There will be no AI jobpocalypse.

item.title: China Thwarts Meta’s Agentic Ambition, U.S. Evaluates Upcoming Models, AI Diagnoses Mammograms
item.description: The Batch AI News and Insights: We’ve been working on AI Andrew, an AI companion shaped by my personality.

item.title: Hermes vs. OpenClaw, Cybersecurity Alarms Ring, More-Interactive Conversations, Can Agents Do Human Work?
item.description: The Batch AI News and Insights: Harvard University just voted to limit the number of A grades given in undergraduate classes to about 20% of the class.

item.title: Gemini Flash Gets Pricey, AI Act Delays, Agents Drive Online Traffic
item.description: The Batch AI News and Insights: One of the new, buzzy jobs in Silicon Valley is the AI Forward Deployed Engineer (FDE), an engineer who is embedded within a client organization to help customize solutions, such as building and tuning agentic workflows that suit the client’s particular needs.

item.title: Qwen3.7-Max Challenges Google for Third Place, AI Saves Whales, Fine-Tuning Breaks Copyright Alignment
item.description: The Batch AI News and Insights: There have been intense efforts over the past few years to lobby governments to pass AI laws for regulatory capture or to suppress open source.

item.title: Mythos Begets Fable, Cursor's Composer 2.5, Agents Building Agents
item.description: The Batch AI News and Insights: If you haven’t already, I encourage you to experiment with using AI agents not just to chat but to actually do work for you on your desktop.

item.title: Testing Mythos and Fable, Moving Beyond SWE-bench, Nvidia's Open Contender
item.description: The Batch AI News and Insights: Over the last two weeks, both the U.S. Government and Anthropic took significant actions that demonstrated their power to control access to AI by restricting what others can do with frontier models.

item.title: A New Generation Studies AI, Apple's Recipe for On-Device Models, GLM5.2 Tackles Open-Ended Problems
item.description: The Batch News & Insights: “Loop engineering” is a hot buzzphrase after Boris Cherney (Claude Code’s creator) and Peter Steinberger (OpenClaw's creator) both mentioned it on social media.
